arts.ttl: ‘At the moment’: Enzo Maresca shares if Chelsea are planning to sell Tosin Adarabioyo in January transfer window
arts.sum: Chelsea have been linked with a number of exits as the January transfer window approaches its final few days. This has included a number of...

arts.ttl: Chelsea transfer news: Blues to hold 'summit' over double deal that could help Man City
arts.sum: Juventus reportedly plan to hold a transfer summit with Chelsea to discuss possible moves for left-back duo Renato Veiga and Ben Chilwell.

arts.ttl: Enzo Maresca claims Chelsea have received PGMOL apology during five-match winless run
arts.sum: Chelsea boss Enzo Maresca claims the PGMOL have been in contact to issue an apology after a decision went against them, thought to be a penalty call in the draw with Crystal Palace

arts.ttl: Chelsea transfer BLUNDER: Blues set to lose money on Joao Felix
arts.sum: AC Milan have reportedly stepped up their interest in Chelsea forward Joao Felix after turning down the chance to sign Manchester United outcast Marcus Rashford. The Serie A giants are only able to sign one non-EU player during the January transfer window, and they have chosen to bring Kyle Walker to the club, rather than Rashford.

arts.ttl: We simulated Chelsea vs Wolves to get a Premier League score prediction
arts.sum: Chelsea will be looking to put an end to their recent run of poor form in the Premier League when they take on relegation-battling Wolves this evening. The draw now leaves Chelsea winless in their last five league games, with Enzo Maresca's side entering the weekend 10 points off top spot.

arts.ttl: Chelsea get Joao Felix AC Milan transfer update as Juventus join Veiga battle
arts.sum: football.london says: It is hard to imagine Chelsea either selling Joao Felix or allowing him to depart for such a significant period on loan just six months on from signing him permanently. Alejandro Garnacho, Jamie Gittens and Mathys Tel have been the attackers linked with Chelsea, while Joao Felix, Renato Veiga and Christopher Nkunku are being targeted by clubs across Europe.

arts.ttl: Player walks out on Wolves transfer after Champions League and Club World Cup turn his head
arts.sum: Over the weekend, multiple reports from the Portuguese media made it clear Manu Silva was on his way to Wolves, in a deal which would bring Vitória de Guimarães around €10-12m. The 23-year-old signed from Feirense and has played 28 matches so far this season. arts.ttl: Report: Chelsea are now interested in signing Arsenal loanee who's been described as 'the best'
arts.sum: He's revived his career with this loan.

arts.ttl: 'Now': Ian Wright tells Raheem Sterling what he needs to do at Arsenal immediately
arts.sum: Since his loan move from Chelsea in the summer, Raheem Sterling’s woes at Arsenal have been quite well documented. When Arsenal secured Sterling on deadline...

arts.ttl: Premier League: 10 talking points from the weekend’s action
arts.sum: Manchester United’s wing-backs are struggling, Foden is back to his best and Bournemouth are top-four contenders Ruben Amorim is repeatedly learning that his team are worryingly susceptible out wide. Brighton’s first two goals came from players being given space on the flanks in the huge gaps between wing-backs and centre-backs. Kaoru Mitoma gifted Yankuba Minteh a tap in and the favour was repaid in the second half. Leny Yoro had terrible troubles against Southampton and there were similar struggles once again as Brighton tested him on the outside. Noussair Mazraoui had a forgettable afternoon, looking poor in possession, which helped Brighton for the opener and he failed to intervene before Mitoma steered the second home at the back post. Mazraoui was moved to the left and soon the third goal arrived as Yasin Ayari was given the freedom to cross the ball. The wing-back positions need specialists and those available to Amorim are unable to provide the defensive robustness and attacking support required, although he will be sticking with 3-4-3 whether they like it or not. Amorim needs to find a solution. Will Unwin Continue reading...
